([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language") **神戸新聞杯**) is a [Grade 2](/wiki/Group_races "Group races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") for three-year-old [Thoroughbreds](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred"). It is run over a distance of 2400 metres at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse") in [Takarazuka, Hyogo](/wiki/Takarazuka,_Hyogo "Takarazuka, Hyogo"), in September, but was contested over 2000 metres until 2006.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
["Kobe Shimbun Hai"](http://japanracing.jp/en/racing/schedule/graded/list/2018/0923kobe-shimbun.html). *Horse Racing in Japan*.
["2016 result"](https://www.racingpost.com/results/491/hanshin/2016-09-25/659903). Racing Post. 25 September 2016.
["2017 result"](https://www.racingpost.com/results/491/hanshin/2017-09-24/685300). Racing Post. 24 September 2017.
["2018 result"](https://www.racingpost.com/results/491/hanshin/2018-09-23/712575). Racing Post. 23 September 2018.
["神戸新聞杯"](https://race.netkeiba.com/special/index.html?id=0097). *Netkeiba*.
["Kobe Shimbun Hai"](https://en.netkeiba.com/race/special.html?id=0097). *Netkeiba*.

神戸新聞杯

The **Kobe Shimbun Hai** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language") **神戸新聞杯**) is a [Grade 2](/wiki/Group_races "Group races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") for three-year-old [Thoroughbreds](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred"). It is run over a distance of 2400 metres at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse") in [Takarazuka, Hyogo](/wiki/Takarazuka,_Hyogo "Takarazuka, Hyogo"), in September, but was contested over 2000 metres until 2006.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The Kobe Shimbun Hai was first run in 1953 and was elevated to Grade 2 status in 1984. It serves as a trial race for the [Kikuka Sho](/wiki/Kikuka_Sho "Kikuka Sho"). Winners of the race have included [Deep Impact](/wiki/Deep_Impact_(horse) "Deep Impact (horse)"), [Zenno Rob Roy](/wiki/Zenno_Rob_Roy "Zenno Rob Roy") and [Orfevre](/wiki/Orfevre "Orfevre").
